raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year> I noticed a transaction on my Citi Bank credit card account for the amount of {$3600.00} from a merchant with the name of XXXX * XXXX XXXX XXXX with the location stating the transaction took place in XXXX. I disputed this immediately. On XX/XX/year> the dispute had seemingly been resolved as my credit card balance was back to a {$0.00} balance as it was before. On XXXX, I noticed the balance was now showing an amount of {$3600.00}. The transaction that caused this shows as : REVERSE XXXX * XXXX XXXX XXXX. After calling Citi 's fraud department, and being transferred all around to different departments, I finally was able to speak to a supervisor named XXXX. She reassured me that yes, this was a mistake and it looks like I have been double charged by this fraudulent merchant. She again reassured me multiple times that she will contact the merchant and get this resolved by Friday, XX/XX/year> and that I have nothing to worry about. All of these conversations are recorded. I never received a call from this supervisor XXXX so I took it upon myself to call Citi on Friday XX/XX/year>. They refused to transfer me to a supervisor upon my request stating that one will call me back in 24-48 business hours. If this fraudulent purchase never occurred, I would have a {$0.00} balance on my credit card. Yet, here we are and I now have a balance of {$3600.00} on my Citi Credit card to no fault of my own and Citi 's refusal to help. This is unacceptable and I will be escalating this until it gets resolved.
